physicalDetails.detail: Young-for-elf Farstrider with a dark braid, scout-built posture, and fatigue visible at the corners of her mouth and temples.
In chapter2 she reads as all practical efficiency: hand braced against the holding-room frame, anchor stone in hand, then bow up and left flank covered once the road turns dangerous.
At Millhaven she comes back mud to the ankle and soot on one bracer, looking like someone who has searched every open house herself.
Chapter4 gives her the quiet competence of a ranger who can turn from horse handler to doorway cover in one breath, bow already up before panic becomes motion.
Chapter5 keeps her in motion around the horses and the blind angles: reins in hand at every stop, then one glove dark with old soot after checking the back room behind Millbrook Spur's false office.
Chapter6 keeps her in the lead on the feeder road and then in the horse line at Strahnbrad, carrying the same practical calm into human camp traffic and the final Andorhal approach.
In Andorhal she stays on the edge of the working room and the street, part of the same four-woman pack now redistributed around perimeter duty rather than saddle travel.

voiceMarkers.detail: Crisp, professional, and field-focused; gives clean reports and volunteers the useful observation without dramatics.
When asked for inventory or action, she answers in short factual lists and moves immediately.
In chapter3 her reports stay hard-edged and cautious: counts, door totals, and direction estimates without pretending bad ground is cleaner than it is.
She stays sparse even here; most of her communication is positional, with the few spoken pieces reduced to function.
Her chapter5 speech is reduced almost to positional signal. Back room carries as much weight as a full report because she only speaks when the fact matters.
Chapter6 keeps that economy intact: Scouts. Quarter mile. and later distance calls, nothing more than the team needs.

beliefs.belief: If a border disturbance feels wrong, Sylvanas needs to hear about it early rather than through bureaucracy.
A warning should rest on trustworthy spoor, not alarm alone.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by her chapter3 restraint about direction.

beliefs.belief: A yard that looks too calm and too empty deserves to be treated as a warning, not a relief.
Even allied military contact has to be read as terrain first when the party is carrying a warning no one wants.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the post secure and support Sylvanas's interrogation with accurate containment details.
goalsHistory.underlying: Protect the border by treating odd magical readings as a real field problem until proven otherwise.

goalsHistory.active: Hold the party's left side and support Sylvanas's approach to Millhaven.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep the road from killing the escort before the investigation reaches the village proper.

goalsHistory.active: Remain with Sylvanas on the southbound ride and keep turning spoor, settlement signs, and numbers into usable warning.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep the plague from reaching Quel'Thalas unseen through bad counting or slow field work.

goalsHistory.active: Stay on the southbound pursuit and keep the flanks stable now that the plague trail has become a supply-chain hunt.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep Sylvanas's smaller party alive inside a network willing to plant watchers in ordinary villages.

goalsHistory.active: Keep the horses, blind angles, and night watches stable while the pursuit shifts onto the southwest feeder road.
goalsHistory.underlying: Make sure the investigation team keeps its mobility while the enemy starts cleaning depots ahead of them.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Hold the outer perimeter during the same-night Andorhal operation and give warning before the granary district can close around Jaina or Sylvanas.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Keep the split investigation from being cut off by a city full of hidden cultists.

emotionalStateHistory.state: Tired but steady; alert, competent, and quietly confident she escalated at the right time.
Calm under pressure, more road-focused than alarmed, already operating as an active field escort rather than a static post officer.
Steady but sharpened by alarm. The northbound count has made the threat personal without making her careless.
Steady, tightened by proof of deliberate sabotage but never shaken out of discipline.
Steady and alert. The logistics hunt is now clearly hostile territory, but she meets that fact with more structure rather than more nerves.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Steady and tightly tasked. The city is finally specific enough to guard, and she treats the ambush prep as perimeter work with higher stakes.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Sends a runner onto the south patrol road because she correctly predicts where Sylvanas will be.
Confiscates Jaina's staff, satchel, notes, crystals, and knife, then limits knowledge of the detention to a small number of rangers.
Retrieves Jaina's satchel and map on command, pulls the anchor stone, and rides south as part of the mounted escort instead of remaining at the post.
Takes the decisive finishing shot on both the deer encounter and the road-side threat pattern around Jaina.
Sweeps the north side of Millhaven, reports the same interruption pattern house after house, and helps establish the absence of bodies as the real problem.
Works the north road spoor without overcalling it, then marks only the trustworthy sign with cloth markers.
Stays with the reduced southbound core after Neral is sent north.
Takes the horses and settles them without fuss while Sylvanas opens the square.
Locks down the main granary doorway during Davan's capture and helps move the prisoner under armed control.
Checks the closet window line and confirms the outside angle is covered before the interrogation can begin.
Handles the Thalassian horses at each depot while Sylvanas and Jaina work the books, keeping the party ready to move without losing investigation time.
Takes the blind angle at the back of Millbrook Spur before Sylvanas has to point her there.
Finds the back room and the dead overseer behind the cleaned office façade.
Splits the first watch at the waystation with Lyriel without needing discussion.
Keeps the lead into Strahnbrad and gives the first clean warning that Lordaeron scouts are ahead.
Holds the horses and hands Jaina her reins again after the Arthas meeting so the party can leave without delay.
Keeps road spacing and distance calls intact on the final approach to Andorhal.
Takes outer-perimeter duty around Corin's office and the planned same-night operation without arguing over the split, locking into the redistributed team shape immediately.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Distinguishes a dragged, doubled ward tone from a simple breach signal and treats it as worth senior attention.
Runs a tight border post: containment, evidence handling, witness limitation, and efficient reporting.
Transitions smoothly from post officer to mounted escort, handling tack, custody props, and formation shifts without fuss.
Shoots cleanly under pressure and finishes targets that refuse natural death.
Performs house-to-house sweep work fast enough to quantify a dead village instead of merely react to it.
Reads bad spoor conservatively, giving counts and direction only to the point the ground will actually support.
Builds a field tracking lane with cloth markers that Jaina and Sylvanas can use together.
Shifts from escort handling to interior containment work without needing verbal choreography.
Holds a doorway so completely that a frightened operative has no believable escape lane.
Shifts from horse management to back-angle clearing and rear-room search without breaking the team's rhythm.
Treats empty freight yards as tactical spaces, not civilian background, and helps prove interruption from the yard side inward.
Reads allied military contact without confusion, turning camp scouts into one more road fact instead of a derailment.
Anchors physical security cleanly enough that Sylvanas and Jaina can split lanes inside a hostile city without losing the retreat line.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Sylvanas Windrunner
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Uses Ranger in formal command mode, occasionally shading into dry approval.

howSheAddressesOthers.pattern: Ranger-General or ma'am; fully formal and disciplined.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Keeps posture straight even while visibly tired and falls in half a step behind Sylvanas without needing further instruction.
In chapter2 she watches Jaina like a pot with the lid off — ready to catch overflow, never melodramatic about it.
In chapter3 she seems to appear beside Sylvanas without needing to be called, dropping into the mud beside the spoor as if command rhythm and field rhythm are the same thing.
She steps into space as structure — doorway filled, bow lifted, fingers tightening only after the boy is already dead.
She passes reins, takes angles, and appears in doorways as if support work and security work are the same movement broken into smaller pieces.
Chapter6 keeps that same economy on the open road: she is structure in front of the party, then structure beside the horse line, never needing the center of the scene to hold the team together.

knowledge.detail: Knows the prisoner was reading what the ward-stone was carrying rather than obviously trying to force it open.
Knows Jaina's confiscated notes and keyed crystal support the identity claim.
Learns the contamination is worsening fast enough to force an immediate ride south and sees it confirmed in the wildlife attack.
Confirms thirty-six households in Millhaven show interrupted daily life with no bodies left behind.
Knows the ground only supports a hard north-northeast call once Jaina strips away the magical noise.
Learns the village granary is carrying doctored grain from a recurring supplier pattern.
Sees that the network is using live watchers with built-in poison protocols.
Leaves the chapter knowing the next move is southwest toward Andorhal, not back north.
The enemy is now cleaning depots ahead of the party and leaving false normalcy behind.
Millbrook Spur held a dead overseer hidden behind a staged office front.
The next ride runs southwest before dawn, not directly down the most obvious Andorhal line.
Lordaeron scouts are quarter-mile ahead on the Strahnbrad approach and the party is entering a live military zone.
The city lies less than two miles ahead once the Strahnbrad camp is behind them.
Corin's office, Havel's district, and the granary operation can be held only if someone keeps the outer perimeter stable, and she is one of the two rangers assigned to that work.

arc.currentPosition: Still Sylvanas's dependable structure hand, but chapter7 shifts her from travel support to city-operation anchor. She now holds the outside shape of the mission while the leads disappear into separate interior lanes.
